quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 2 Quiz 7
quiz.subtitle: Postulates Regarding Parallel Lines and Transversals
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: A transversal is defined as:
options: A line that is parallel to two other lines in the same plane
A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points
A line that is perpendicular to exactly one other line
A line that bisects the angle formed by two intersecting lines
questions.explanation: A transversal is a line that intersects two or more coplanar lines at different points. The angles formed by this intersection are the subject of the parallel line postulates and theorems.

questions.question: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, which pair of angles are always congruent?
options: Adjacent angles on the same side of the transversal
questions.explanation: The Corresponding Angles Postulate states that if two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, then corresponding angles are congruent. Corresponding angles occupy the same position relative to the intersection points.

questions.question: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, which pair of angles are always supplementary?
questions.explanation: Consecutive interior angles (also called same-side interior angles) are supplementary when the lines are parallel — their measures sum to $180^\circ$. Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are congruent, not supplementary.

questions.question: In the figure, $m\angle 1 = 72^\circ$. Lines $\ell$ and $m$ are parallel. $\angle 1$ and $\angle 5$ are corresponding angles. What is $m\angle 5$?
questions.explanation: By the Corresponding Angles Postulate, if lines are parallel, corresponding angles are congruent. Since $\angle 1$ and $\angle 5$ are corresponding angles, $m\angle 5 = m\angle 1 = 72^\circ$.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. $\angle 2$ and $\angle 7$ are alternate exterior angles. If $m\angle 2 = 115^\circ$, what is $m\angle 7$?
questions.explanation: The Alternate Exterior Angles Theorem states that if two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, then alternate exterior angles are congruent. So $m\angle 7 = m\angle 2 = 115^\circ$.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. $\angle 3$ and $\angle 6$ are alternate interior angles. If $m\angle 3 = 78^\circ$, what is $m\angle 6$?
questions.explanation: The Alternate Interior Angles Theorem states that if two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, then alternate interior angles are congruent. So $m\angle 6 = m\angle 3 = 78^\circ$.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. $\angle 4$ and $\angle 5$ are consecutive interior angles. If $m\angle 4 = 63^\circ$, what is $m\angle 5$?
questions.explanation: The Consecutive Interior Angles Theorem states that if two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, then consecutive interior angles are supplementary. So $m\angle 5 = 180^\circ - 63^\circ = 117^\circ$.

questions.question: Two lines are cut by a transversal. A pair of alternate interior angles are found to be congruent. Which conclusion is valid?
options: The lines must be perpendicular to the transversal
The lines must intersect on the same side of the transversal
The angles must also be supplementary
questions.explanation: The converse of the Alternate Interior Angles Theorem states that if alternate interior angles are congruent, then the lines cut by the transversal are parallel.

questions.question: Two lines are cut by a transversal. A pair of consecutive interior angles are supplementary. Which conclusion is valid?
options: The transversal is perpendicular to both lines
The angles form a linear pair with the transversal
The alternate exterior angles are also supplementary
questions.explanation: The converse of the Consecutive Interior Angles Theorem states that if consecutive interior angles are supplementary, then the lines cut by the transversal are parallel.

questions.question: Two lines are cut by a transversal, and corresponding angles are congruent. Why is this sufficient to conclude the lines are parallel?
options: Corresponding angles are only formed when lines are already proven parallel
The Corresponding Angles Postulate works as both a theorem and its own converse
Congruent corresponding angles imply the lines have equal slopes, which defines parallel lines
Corresponding angle congruence is unrelated to parallelism in Euclidean geometry
questions.explanation: The Corresponding Angles Postulate is typically presented as a postulate in one direction (if lines are parallel, corresponding angles are congruent) and its converse is also accepted: if corresponding angles are congruent, the lines are parallel.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the angle relationships formed by parallel lines and a transversal, including their converses.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the main theorems but review the distinction between congruent pairs (corresponding, alternate interior, alternate exterior) and supplementary pairs (consecutive interior).
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: corresponding angles are congruent, alternate interior angles are congruent, alternate exterior angles are congruent, and consecutive interior angles are supplementary.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! When parallel lines are cut by a transversal: corresponding angles are equal, alternate interior angles are equal, alternate exterior angles are equal, and same-side interior angles sum to $180^\circ$.
